sb_prompt: PLEASE DISCUSS MUSIC.  CAN YOU FIND MUSICIANSOR TYPES OF MUSIC THAT BOTH YOU AND THE OTHER CALLER LIKE?
turns: So, what kind of music do you usually listen to? I'm always on the lookout for new artists and genres.
Lately, I've been into indie rock and alternative music. Artists like Phoebe Bridgers, Mitski, and Arctic Monkeys have been on heavy rotation for me.
Oh, those are great choices! I also love Phoebe Bridlers's voice and the emotional depth in her lyrics.
Definitely! Have you listened to any other female singer-songwriters that you would recommend?
Yes, I really enjoy the work of Lucy Dacus and Julien Baker.
Thanks for the suggestions! I'll definitely check them out. What about hip hop or electronic music?
Sure thing! For electronic music, I'd suggest listening to Grimes - she has this ethereal, dreamy quality to her sound.
Interesting choice. Are there any specific tracks or albums from Grimes you'd recommend starting with?
Her album "Visions" is a good place to start if you want to explore her discography.
Great, I'll give it a listen. As for hip hop, who do you think is worth checking out these days?
Recently, I've been enjoying J. Cole's album "KOD." It delves into personal experiences while also addressing broader societal issues.
That sounds interesting. Any particular tracks or songs you'd recommend from "KOD"?
My favorite track from that album is "1985 (Intro to 'The Fall Off')," which sets an introspective tone for the rest of the album.
Got it, thanks for the tip! Is there any genre or era of music that you feel hasn't received enough attention?

I think jazz from the mid-century deserves more recognition. There were so many incredible musicians during that time, like Miles Davis and Billie Holiday, whose contributions to music should be celebrated more widely.
Absolutely agree, jazz from that period had such a profound impact on music as a whole.
Exactly! Do you have a favorite jazz artist or album you'd like to share?
One of my favorites is Thelonious Monk, especially his album "Brilliant Corners." The unique harmonic structure and inventive playing style make it a standout recording in the jazz world.
Ah, yes! "Brilliant Corners" is a fantastic album.
Glad we both appreciate it! To wrap up, do you have any concert or festival experiences that stood out to you?
A few years ago, I went to see Bon Iver at a small outdoor venue.
It was absolutely magical. The setting sun created this beautiful ambiance, and Justin Vernon's falsetto voice carried so much emotion throughout the performance.
Sounds like a memorable experience. Music truly brings people together in special ways like that.
Couldn't agree more. Thank you for sharing your musical preferences with me.
Likewise, it was great discussing music with you! Enjoy exploring the new artists and genres we talked about.
You too! Take care and happy listening! Bye!
Bye, have a great day! Happy listening! :) :) :) :) :)
